papers.title: Federated Learning for Medical Image Analysis: A Review
papers.summary: This paper presents a comprehensive review of federated learning applications in medical imaging. It discusses privacy-preserving techniques and their effectiveness across multiple hospital domains.
papers.contribution: Provides a taxonomy of privacy-preserving techniques specific to medical imaging constraints.

papers.title: Robust Federated Learning with Noisy Labels in Healthcare
papers.summary: The authors propose a robust federated learning framework that handles noisy labels effectively. The approach is validated on multi-center electronic health records.
papers.contribution: Introduced a novel robust aggregation method that outperforms FedAvg by 15% on datasets with noisy labels.

papers.title: Differential Privacy in Medical Federated Learning
papers.summary: Explores the trade-off between model utility and privacy guarantees when applying differential privacy to federated clinical workflows.
papers.contribution: Demonstrates that adaptive clipping significantly improves convergence on heterogeneous medical data.

papers.title: Handling Missing Modalities in Centralized Medical Imaging
papers.summary: This work addresses the problem of missing MRI sequences in single-hospital setups using generative adversarial networks to impute missing data.
papers.contribution: A novel cross-modal attention mechanism that robustly synthesizes missing T1 or T2 scans from available modalities.

papers.title: Federated Semi-Supervised Learning for COVID-19 Detection
papers.summary: Leverages unlabelled CT scans from multiple hospitals alongside a small set of annotated data to create a robust COVID-19 screening tool.
papers.contribution: A pseudo-labeling strategy optimized for non-IID data distributions across clients.

papers.title: Communication-Efficient Federated Learning for EHR Data
papers.summary: Proposes a compression technique specifically designed for high-dimensional but sparse electronic health record data to reduce communication overhead.
papers.contribution: Achieves 40x communication reduction with negligible drop in prediction accuracy for patient readmission.

papers.title: Personalized Federated Learning in Oncology
papers.summary: A framework that allows individual hospitals to maintain personalized models while still benefiting from shared representations in rare cancer detection.
papers.contribution: Introduced a decoupled representation learning approach that separates the base extractor from the hospital-specific classifier.

papers.title: Secure Multiparty Computation for Healthcare Analytics
papers.summary: Compares federated learning against traditional secure multiparty computation for multi-institutional healthcare analytics on tabular data.
papers.contribution: Provides a comprehensive benchmarking suite showing FL is 100x faster than SMPC for neural networks, though with weaker formal privacy.

papers.title: Fairness in Federated Learning for Dermatology
papers.summary: Identifies bias in multi-hospital dermatology datasets where skin tone representation is highly skewed across different geographic clients.
papers.contribution: A fairness-aware aggregation rule that explicitly balances performance across diverse racial demographics.
